# YAAF-Common — Complete Reference

> Comprehensive, code-verified documentation for AI coding assistants.
> Every signature here was taken from the actual source in this repository. If you generate code against this library, follow these signatures exactly — several "obvious" API shapes (a `NewQuery` constructor, a Redis-like `cache.Set(key, bytes)`, `Subscribe(topic, handler)`) do NOT exist here. When unsure, open the referenced file.

## Table of contents

1. Project overview
2. Architecture & design principles
3. Entity system
4. Database interfaces (IDatabase, IDatastore)
5. Query builder (IQuery, QueryFilter, operators)
6. Cache interface (IDataCache)
7. Messaging system
8. Configuration
9. Logging
10. Utilities
11. Testing patterns
12. Worked use cases
13. Common mistakes to avoid

---

## 1. Project overview

- **Repository**: github.com/go-yaaf/yaaf-common
- **Language**: Go 1.24.0+
- **License**: Apache 2.0
- **Dependencies**: github.com/google/uuid v1.6.0, github.com/wneessen/go-mail v0.7.2, golang.org/x/text (indirect)

YAAF-Common is the foundational library for the YAAF ecosystem. It provides:
- Standard interfaces for infrastructure (DB, datastore, cache, message bus).
- In-memory implementations of each interface for tests and local development.
- An entity model and ID generators.
- Utility packages (collections, worker pools, hashing, mail, nanoid, etc.).

Concrete production adapters (PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, Kafka, Google Pub/Sub, BigQuery, ...) live in **separate** `go-yaaf/*` modules and implement the interfaces defined here.

### Key files

| File | Defines |
|------|---------|
| `entity/entity.go` | `Entity`, `BaseEntity`, `BaseEntityEx`, `BaseAnalyticEntity`, `SimpleEntity[T]`, `Entities[T]`, `EntityFactory`, ID generators |
| `entity/timestamp.go` | `Timestamp` (`int64`, epoch **milliseconds**) |
| `database/database.go` | `IDatabase` |
| `database/datastore.go` | `IDatastore` |
| `database/datacache.go` | `IDataCache`, `ILocker` |
| `database/query.go` | `IQuery`, `AggFunc` |
| `database/query_filter.go` | `QueryFilter`, `F(...)`, `Filter(...)` |
| `database/query_operator.go` | `QueryOperator` constants |
| `database/in_memory_*.go` | `NewInMemoryDatabase/Datastore/DataCache` |
| `messaging/message_bus.go` | `IMessageBus`, `IMessageProducer`, `IMessageConsumer` |
| `messaging/messages.go` | `IMessage`, `BaseMessage`, `Message[T]`, `MessageFactory`, `SubscriptionCallback` |
| `config/base_config.go` | `BaseConfig`, `config.Get()` |
| `logger/logger.go` | slog-based logger |

---

## 2. Architecture & design principles

1. **Program to interfaces.** Inject `IDatabase`/`IDataCache`/`IMessageBus`, never concrete structs.
2. **Every interface has an in-memory implementation.** Use them in tests; swap the production adapter in `main`.
3. **Entity-centric.** All persisted/messaged domain types implement `Entity` and are created through an `EntityFactory` so the library can allocate typed instances during deserialization.
4. **Explicit over magic.** Filters, sorts, sharding keys, and factories are passed explicitly.

### Dependency injection

```go
type UserService struct {
    db    database.IDatabase
    cache database.IDataCache
    bus   messaging.IMessageBus
}

func NewUserService(db database.IDatabase, cache database.IDataCache, bus messaging.IMessageBus) *UserService {
    return &UserService{db: db, cache: cache, bus: bus}
}
```

---

## 3. Entity system

### Entity interface (`entity/entity.go`)

```go
type Entity interface {
    ID() string     // Unique identifier
    TABLE() string  // Table/collection name; may contain template variables (see below)
    NAME() string   // Human-readable name (default: "<TABLE> <Id>"), used in logs
    KEY() string    // Sharding key (e.g. tenant/account id); default ""
}

type EntityFactory func() Entity
```

### BaseEntity

```go
type BaseEntity struct {
    Id        string    `json:"id"`
    CreatedOn Timestamp `json:"createdOn"` // epoch milliseconds
    UpdatedOn Timestamp `json:"updatedOn"` // epoch milliseconds
}
```

Embed it and implement `TABLE()`. `ID()`, `NAME()`, `KEY()` come from `BaseEntity`; override as needed.

```go
type User struct {
    entity.BaseEntity
    Name  string `json:"name"`
    Email string `json:"email"`
}

func (u *User) TABLE() string { return "user" }

func NewUser() entity.Entity { return &User{} } // factory
```

### BaseEntityEx

Adds status flag + free-form properties, for soft-delete and extensible records:

```go
type BaseEntityEx struct {
    Id        string    `json:"id"`
    CreatedOn Timestamp `json:"createdOn"`
    UpdatedOn Timestamp `json:"updatedOn"`
    Flag      int64     `json:"flag"`  // convention: -1 = deleted, 0 = active, >0 = custom states
    Props     Json      `json:"props"` // Json = map[string]any
}
```

Other helpers in `entity/entity.go`: `BaseAnalyticEntity` (append-only analytic/event rows), `SimpleEntity[T any]` (wrap a primitive as an entity), `Entities[T any]` (typed collection). `Json` is `map[string]any`. `Timestamp` is `int64` epoch **milliseconds** (`entity/timestamp.go`).

### ID generators (package-level functions, not methods)

```go
ID()               // long alphanumeric, base-36 of epoch microseconds — time-sortable
IDN()              // long numeric string, epoch microseconds — time-sortable
ShortID(delta ...int)  // ~6-char alphanumeric, base-36 of epoch seconds
ShortIDN(delta ...int) // short numeric string, epoch seconds
NanoID()           // 21-char URL-friendly id (falls back to time-based on failure)
GUID()             // UUID v4 (RFC 4122)
```

Typical use — assign in a constructor:
```go
func NewUserEntity(name string) *User {
    u := &User{Name: name}
    u.Id = entity.ID()
    return u
}
```

### Table templates & sharding

`TABLE()` may embed template variables resolved at access time:
- `{{tenantId}}` / `{{accountId}}` — from `KEY()` / the passed sharding key
- `{{year}}` — current year (e.g. `2026`)
- `{{month}}` — current month (`01`–`12`)

```go
func (e *Event) TABLE() string { return "events_{{accountId}}_{{year}}_{{month}}" }
func (e *Event) KEY() string   { return e.AccountId } // used to resolve {{accountId}} and for sharding
```

The `keys ...string` variadic on database/cache methods supplies the sharding key(s) for the operation.

---

## 4. Database interfaces

### IDatabase (`database/database.go`)

```go
type IDatabase interface {
    io.Closer
    Ping(retries uint, intervalInSeconds uint) error
    CloneDatabase() (IDatabase, error)

    // Single-entity CRUD
    Get(factory EntityFactory, entityID string, keys ...string) (result Entity, err error)
    List(factory EntityFactory, entityIDs []string, keys ...string) (list []Entity, err error)
    Exists(factory EntityFactory, entityID string, keys ...string) (result bool, err error)
    Insert(entity Entity) (added Entity, err error)
    Update(entity Entity) (updated Entity, err error)
    Upsert(entity Entity) (updated Entity, err error)
    Delete(factory EntityFactory, entityID string, keys ...string) (err error)

    // Bulk
    BulkInsert(entities []Entity) (affected int64, err error)
    BulkUpdate(entities []Entity) (affected int64, err error)
    BulkUpsert(entities []Entity) (affected int64, err error)
    BulkDelete(factory EntityFactory, entityIDs []string, keys ...string) (affected int64, err error)

    // Field-level updates
    SetField(factory EntityFactory, entityID string, field string, value any, keys ...string) (err error)
    SetFields(factory EntityFactory, entityID string, fields map[string]any, keys ...string) (err error)
    BulkSetFields(factory EntityFactory, field string, values map[string]any, keys ...string) (affected int64, err error)

    // Querying
    Query(factory EntityFactory) IQuery
    AdvancedQuery(factory EntityFactory) IAdvancedQuery

    // DDL / raw SQL
    ExecuteDDL(ddl map[string][]string) (err error)
    ExecuteSQL(sql string, args ...any) (affected int64, err error)
    ExecuteQuery(source, sql string, args ...any) ([]Json, error)
    DropTable(table string) (err error)
    PurgeTable(table string) (err error)
}
```

Notes:
- `Get`/`List`/`Delete` take an `EntityFactory` (not a pointer receiver). Results come back as `Entity`; type-assert to your concrete type: `user := e.(*User)`.
- There is **no** `db.Execute(query, ...)` and **no** `db.Find(query, &out)`. Execution happens through the `IQuery` methods (`Find`, `List`, `Count`, ...). See §5.

### IDatastore (`database/datastore.go`)

Same programming model as `IDatabase` for NoSQL document stores (collections instead of tables, JSON documents, flexible schema). It also exposes `Query(factory) IQuery`, CRUD, bulk, and field updates. Constructor: `NewInMemoryDatastore() (IDatastore, error)`.

### In-memory constructors (no arguments)

```go
db, err    := database.NewInMemoryDatabase()   // (IDatabase, error)
ds, err    := database.NewInMemoryDatastore()  // (IDatastore, error)
cache, err := database.NewInMemoryDataCache()   // (IDataCache, error)  ← takes NO expiration args
```

---

## 5. Query builder

Get an `IQuery` from `db.Query(factory)` (or `ds.Query(factory)`), chain builder methods, then call a terminal method.

### IQuery (`database/query.go`)

```go
type IQuery interface {
    // Builder (chainable)
    Apply(cb func(in Entity) Entity) IQuery
    Filter(filter QueryFilter) IQuery
    Range(field string, from Timestamp, to Timestamp) IQuery
    MatchAll(filters ...QueryFilter) IQuery   // AND
    MatchAny(filters ...QueryFilter) IQuery   // OR
    Sort(sort string) IQuery                  // "field" asc, "field-" desc
    Page(page int) IQuery                     // 0-based
    Limit(pageSize int) IQuery

    // Terminal / execution
    List(entityIDs []string, keys ...string) (out []Entity, err error)
    Find(keys ...string) (out []Entity, total int64, err error)
    FindSingle(keys ...string) (entity Entity, err error)
    Select(fields ...string) ([]Json, error)
    Count(keys ...string) (total int64, err error)
    GetMap(keys ...string) (out map[string]Entity, err error)
    GetIDs(keys ...string) (out []string, err error)
    Delete(keys ...string) (total int64, err error)
    SetField(field string, value any, keys ...string) (total int64, err error)
    SetFields(fields map[string]any, keys ...string) (total int64, err error)

    // Aggregations
    Aggregation(field string, function AggFunc, keys ...string) (value float64, err error)
    GroupCount(field string, keys ...string) (out map[any]int64, total int64, err error)
    GroupAggregation(field string, function AggFunc, keys ...string) (out map[any]Tuple[int64, float64], total float64, err error)
    Histogram(field string, function AggFunc, timeField string, interval time.Duration, keys ...string) (out map[Timestamp]Tuple[int64, float64], total float64, err error)
    Histogram2D(field string, function AggFunc, dim, timeField string, interval time.Duration, keys ...string) (out map[Timestamp]map[any]Tuple[int64, float64], total float64, err error)

    ToString() string
}
```

Key facts:
- **No `NewQuery(...)` constructor.** Always start from `db.Query(factory)`.
- `Filter` takes a `QueryFilter` value (built with `F(...)`), not `(field, op, value)`.
- Multiple `.Filter(...)` calls AND together; or use `MatchAll(...)` / `MatchAny(...)`.
- `Sort("name")` = ascending; `Sort("name-")` (trailing dash) = descending.
- `Find` returns `(out []Entity, total int64, err error)` — three values.

### QueryFilter (`database/query_filter.go`)

Build filters with `F(field)` (or its alias `Filter(field)`) then an operator method:

```go
F("age").Gte(30)
F("status").Eq("active")
F("role").In("admin", "editor")
F("createdOn").Between(from, to)
F("name").Like("jo%")
F("flag").WithFlag(2)        // bitwise AND against flag
F("deleted").IsFalse()
F("email").IsEmpty()
F("age").Gte(18).If(applyAgeFilter)  // .If(false) deactivates the filter
```

Operator methods (each returns `QueryFilter`, chainable): `Eq`, `Neq`, `Like`, `NotLike`, `Gt`, `Gte`, `Lt`, `Lte`, `In`, `NotIn`, `InSubQuery`, `NotInSubQuery`, `Between`, `Contains`, `NotContains`, `WithFlag`, `WithNoFlag`, `IsEmpty`, `IsTrue`, `IsFalse`, and `If(bool)` (conditionally activate).

### QueryOperator constants (`database/query_operator.go`)

`Eq`, `Neq`, `Like`, `NotLike`, `Gt`, `Gte`, `Lt`, `Lte`, `In`, `NotIn`, `InSQ`, `NotInSQ`, `Between`, `Contains`, `NotContains`, `Empty`, `True`, `False`, `WithFlag`, `WithNoFlag`. You rarely reference these directly — prefer the `F(...)` builder methods.

### Full example

```go
list, total, err := db.Query(NewUser).
    MatchAll(
        database.F("status").Eq("active"),
        database.F("age").Gte(30),
    ).
    Sort("name").   // ascending
    Page(0).        // first page
    Limit(20).
    Find()          // (out []Entity, total int64, err error)

for _, e := range list {
    u := e.(*User)
    _ = u
}
```

---

## 6. Cache interface (IDataCache)

`IDataCache` is **entity-oriented**, not a raw Redis client. Values are `Entity` (serialized by the implementation) with parallel `*Raw` methods for byte slices. Distinct method names: `Del` (not `Delete`), `Scan` (not `Keys`).

```go
type IDataCache interface {
    io.Closer
    Ping(retries uint, intervalInSeconds uint) error
    CloneDataCache() (IDataCache, error)

    // Keys (entity + raw variants)
    Get(factory EntityFactory, key string) (Entity, error)
    GetRaw(key string) ([]byte, error)
    GetKeys(factory EntityFactory, keys ...string) ([]Entity, error)
    GetRawKeys(keys ...string) ([]Tuple[string, []byte], error)
    Set(key string, entity Entity, expiration ...time.Duration) error
    SetRaw(key string, bytes []byte, expiration ...time.Duration) error
    SetNX(key string, entity Entity, expiration ...time.Duration) (bool, error)   // set if absent
    SetRawNX(key string, bytes []byte, expiration ...time.Duration) (bool, error)
    Add(key string, entity Entity, expiration time.Duration) (bool, error)         // alias of SetNX
    AddRaw(key string, bytes []byte, expiration time.Duration) (bool, error)
    Del(keys ...string) error
    Rename(key string, newKey string) error
    Exists(key string) (bool, error)
    Scan(from uint64, match string, count int64) (keys []string, cursor uint64, err error)

    // Hashes
    HGet(factory EntityFactory, key, field string) (Entity, error)
    HGetRaw(key, field string) ([]byte, error)
    HKeys(key string) ([]string, error)
    HGetAll(factory EntityFactory, key string) (map[string]Entity, error)
    HGetRawAll(key string) (map[string][]byte, error)
    HSet(key, field string, entity Entity) error
    HSetRaw(key, field string, bytes []byte) error
    HSetNX(key, field string, entity Entity) (bool, error)
    HSetRawNX(key, field string, bytes []byte) (bool, error)
    HDel(key string, fields ...string) error
    HAdd(key, field string, entity Entity) (bool, error)
    HAddRaw(key, field string, bytes []byte) (bool, error)
    HExists(key, field string) (bool, error)

    // Lists
    RPush(key string, value ...Entity) error
    LPush(key string, value ...Entity) error
    RPop(factory EntityFactory, key string) (Entity, error)
    LPop(factory EntityFactory, key string) (Entity, error)
    BRPop(factory EntityFactory, timeout time.Duration, keys ...string) (key string, entity Entity, err error)
    BLPop(factory EntityFactory, timeout time.Duration, keys ...string) (key string, entity Entity, err error)
    LRange(factory EntityFactory, key string, start, stop int64) ([]Entity, error)
    LLen(key string) int64

    // Distributed lock
    ObtainLocker(key string, ttl time.Duration) (ILocker, error)
}

type ILocker interface {
    Key() string
    Token() string
    TTL(ctx context.Context) (time.Duration, error)
    Refresh(ctx context.Context, ttl time.Duration) error
    Release(ctx context.Context) error
}
```

Usage:
```go
cache, _ := database.NewInMemoryDataCache()

_ = cache.Set("user:"+u.ID(), u, 5*time.Minute)
e, err := cache.Get(NewUser, "user:"+u.ID())
if err == nil {
    u := e.(*User)
    _ = u
}

// distributed lock
lk, err := cache.ObtainLocker("job:nightly", 30*time.Second)
if err == nil {
    defer lk.Release(ctx)
    // critical section
}
```

> The in-process generic caches in `utils/cache` / `utils/cache2` are a **different** thing (with `GetOrDefault`/`Val`). They are not `IDataCache` and are not backed by Redis.

---

## 7. Messaging system

### IMessageBus (`messaging/message_bus.go`)

```go
type IMessageBus interface {
    io.Closer
    Ping(retries uint, intervalInSeconds uint) error
    CloneMessageBus() (IMessageBus, error)

    // Pub/Sub
    Publish(messages ...IMessage) error
    Subscribe(subscription string, mf MessageFactory, callback SubscriptionCallback, topics ...string) (string, error)
    Unsubscribe(subscriptionId string) bool

    // Queue
    Push(messages ...IMessage) error
    Pop(mf MessageFactory, timeout time.Duration, queue ...string) (IMessage, error)

    // Producer / consumer
    CreateProducer(topic string) (IMessageProducer, error)
    CreateConsumer(subscription string, mf MessageFactory, topics ...string) (IMessageConsumer, error)
}
```

`IMessageProducer`: `Close() error`, `Publish(messages ...IMessage) error`.
`IMessageConsumer`: `Close() error`, `Read(timeout time.Duration) (IMessage, error)`.

### IMessage & message types (`messaging/messages.go`)

```go
type IMessage interface {
    Topic() string      // channel/queue name
    OpCode() int        // operation code / message type discriminator
    Addressee() string  // optional recipient
    SessionId() string  // correlates related messages
    Version() string    // message format version
    Payload() any       // body
}

type MessageFactory func() IMessage
type SubscriptionCallback func(msg IMessage) bool // return true to ack
```

Build messages with the generics; embed `BaseMessage` for custom types:

```go
type Message[T any] struct {
    BaseMessage
    MsgPayload T `json:"payload"`
}

NewMessage[T any]() IMessage                        // empty typed message (use as factory body)
GetMessage[T any](topic string, payload T) IMessage // typed message with payload + generated SessionId
```

### Pub/Sub example

```go
bus, _ := messaging.NewInMemoryMessageBus()

// factory tells the bus how to allocate an incoming message for deserialization
factory := func() messaging.IMessage { return messaging.NewMessage[*User]() }

sub, _ := bus.Subscribe("user-service", factory, func(m messaging.IMessage) bool {
    u := m.Payload().(*User)
    _ = u
    return true // ack; return false to nack/redeliver
}, "user.created")

_ = bus.Publish(messaging.GetMessage[*User]("user.created", newUser))

_ = bus.Unsubscribe(sub)
```

### Queue example

```go
_ = bus.Push(messaging.GetMessage[*Task]("tasks", task))
factory := func() messaging.IMessage { return messaging.NewMessage[*Task]() }
msg, err := bus.Pop(factory, 5*time.Second, "tasks")
```

> `Subscribe` is `(subscription, factory, callback, topics...)` — NOT `(topic, handler)`. `Publish`/`Push` are variadic. `NewMessage` is generic and takes no topic/payload — set those via `GetMessage[T](topic, payload)`.

---

## 8. Configuration (`config/base_config.go`)

`BaseConfig` is a singleton fed from environment variables. There is **no** `NewConfig()`; use `config.Get()`:

```go
cfg := config.Get() // initializes once, scans env vars

// typed getters with defaults
level := cfg.GetStringParamValueOrDefault("LOG_LEVEL", "INFO")
n     := cfg.GetIntParamValueOrDefault("MAX_WORKERS", 8)
flag  := cfg.GetBoolParamValueOrDefault("UNDER_TEST", false)

// named accessors (read specific well-known variables)
dbURI    := cfg.DatabaseUri()
cacheURI := cfg.DataCacheUri()
mqURI    := cfg.MessagingUri()
```

Other accessors include `DatastoreUri()`, `StreamingUri()`, `BigQueryUri()`, `LogLevel()`, `EnableLogJsonFormat()`, `HttpReadTimeoutMs()`, `PubSubNumOfGoroutines()`, `MaxDbConnections()`, `UnderTest()`, and many WebSocket/PubSub tuning values. Register/override values programmatically with `AddConfigVar(key, value)` and re-scan with `ScanEnvVariables()`.

To build a service-specific config, embed `*config.BaseConfig` and add your own typed getters that read named variables.

---

## 9. Logging (`logger/logger.go`)

Structured logging on top of Go's standard `log/slog` (not zap).

```go
import "github.com/go-yaaf/yaaf-common/logger"

logger.SetLevel("DEBUG")       // "debug" | "info" | "warn"/"warning" | "error"
logger.EnableJsonFormat(true)  // JSON vs text
logger.EnableCaller(true)      // include source file:line
logger.SetTimeLayout(time.RFC3339)
logger.Init()                  // (re)initialize

logger.Debug("processing %d items", n)
logger.Info("service started on port %d", port)
logger.Warn("missing config %s, using default", key)
logger.Error("db error: %v", err)
logger.Fatal("cannot start: %v", err) // logs then os.Exit(1)
```

All log functions are printf-style: `func(format string, params ...any)`. `EnableStacktrace(bool)` exists but is a no-op under slog.

---

## 10. Utilities (`utils/`)

- **collections/** — thread-safe `SafeMap`/concurrent maps, sets, queues, stacks, ring buffers.
- **pool/** — worker pool for bounded concurrency.
- **cache/ & cache2/** — in-process generic caches (include `GetOrDefault`, `Val`). Not `IDataCache`.
- **nanoid/** — compact URL-friendly ID generation (backs `entity.NanoID()`).
- **binary/** — byte/bit manipulation and encoding helpers.
- **mail/** — email sending via `wneessen/go-mail`.
- **Aggregator/** — data aggregation helpers.
- Top-level files: `hash_utils.go`, `http_utils.go`, `json_utils.go`, `string_utils.go`, `time_utils.go`, `docker_utils.go`, `recover.go`, `print_banner.go`.

Sibling top-level packages: `rest/` (REST message helpers), `metrics/` (metrics utilities), `files/` (file + file-store abstractions: local and HTTP-backed).

---

## 11. Testing patterns

Use the in-memory implementations — no external services required:

```go
func TestUserService(t *testing.T) {
    db, _ := database.NewInMemoryDatabase()
    cache, _ := database.NewInMemoryDataCache()
    bus, _ := messaging.NewInMemoryMessageBus()
    defer db.Close()
    defer cache.Close()
    defer bus.Close()

    svc := NewUserService(db, cache, bus)

    added, err := db.Insert(&User{Name: "Test"})
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("insert: %v", err)
    }

    e, err := db.Get(NewUser, added.ID())
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("get: %v", err)
    }
    if got := e.(*User).Name; got != "Test" {
        t.Errorf("want Test, got %s", got)
    }
}
```

```bash
go test ./...          # all
go test ./database     # one package
go test -cover ./...   # coverage
go test -v ./...       # verbose
```

The `test/` directory holds shared sample entities and integration tests.

---

## 12. Worked use cases

### CRUD
```go
db, _ := database.NewInMemoryDatabase()

added, _ := db.Insert(&Product{Name: "Laptop", Price: 999.99})
e, _ := db.Get(func() entity.Entity { return &Product{} }, added.ID())
p := e.(*Product)
p.Price = 899.99
_, _ = db.Update(p)
_ = db.Delete(func() entity.Entity { return &Product{} }, p.ID())
```

### Partial + bulk updates
```go
_ = db.SetField(NewUser, id, "status", "active")
_ = db.SetFields(NewUser, id, map[string]any{"status": "active", "age": 31})

_, _ = db.BulkInsert([]entity.Entity{&User{Name: "A"}, &User{Name: "B"}})
_, _ = db.BulkSetFields(NewUser, "status", map[string]any{
    "user1": "active", "user2": "inactive",
})
```

### Cache-aside read
```go
key := "user:" + id
if e, err := cache.Get(NewUser, key); err == nil && e != nil {
    return e.(*User), nil
}
e, err := db.Get(NewUser, id)
if err != nil {
    return nil, err
}
_ = cache.Set(key, e, 5*time.Minute)
return e.(*User), nil
```

### Event-driven flow
```go
factory := func() messaging.IMessage { return messaging.NewMessage[*User]() }
bus.Subscribe("audit", factory, func(m messaging.IMessage) bool {
    logger.Info("user created: %s", m.Payload().(*User).ID())
    return true
}, "user.created")

bus.Publish(messaging.GetMessage[*User]("user.created", newUser))
```

### Multi-tenant sharding
```go
type Reading struct {
    entity.BaseEntity
    AccountId string  `json:"accountId"`
    Value     float64 `json:"value"`
}

func (r *Reading) TABLE() string { return "readings_{{accountId}}_{{year}}_{{month}}" }
func (r *Reading) KEY() string   { return r.AccountId }

// pass the shard key explicitly on reads:
e, _ := db.Get(func() entity.Entity { return &Reading{} }, id, accountId)
```

### Soft delete with BaseEntityEx
```go
type Item struct {
    entity.BaseEntityEx
    Name string `json:"name"`
}
func (i *Item) TABLE() string { return "item" }

item.Flag = -1                      // mark deleted
_, _ = db.Update(item)

// query only active:
list, _, _ := db.Query(func() entity.Entity { return &Item{} }).
    Filter(database.F("flag").Gte(0)).
    Find()
```

---

## 13. Common mistakes to avoid

These are the API shapes assistants most often hallucinate for this library. All of the "wrong" forms below will NOT compile.

| Don't write | Do write |
|-------------|----------|
| `database.NewQuery("users")` | `db.Query(NewUser)` |
| `.WithFilter("age", database.Gte, 30)` | `.Filter(database.F("age").Gte(30))` |
| `.WithSort("name", true)` | `.Sort("name")` (asc) / `.Sort("name-")` (desc) |
| `db.Execute(query, factory)` / `db.Find(q, &out)` | `query.Find()` → `(out []Entity, total int64, err error)` |
| `db.Get(id, &user)` | `e, err := db.Get(NewUser, id)`; `user := e.(*User)` |
| `database.NewInMemoryDataCache(5*time.Minute, 10*time.Minute)` | `database.NewInMemoryDataCache()` (no args) |
| `cache.Set(key, []byte(v))` | `cache.Set(key, entity, exp...)` or `cache.SetRaw(key, bytes, exp...)` |
| `cache.Delete(key)` / `cache.Keys(pat)` | `cache.Del(key)` / `cache.Scan(from, match, count)` |
| `cache.Increment(key, 1)` | (not on `IDataCache`) — use hash/list ops or a concrete adapter |
| `bus.Subscribe(topic, handler)` | `bus.Subscribe(sub, factory, callback, topics...)` |
| `messaging.NewMessage(topic, payload)` | `messaging.GetMessage[T](topic, payload)`; factory: `messaging.NewMessage[T]()` |
| `config.NewConfig("app")` | `config.Get()` |
| logging via `zap` | Go `log/slog` (`logger.Info(format, args...)`) |

General rules:
1. Always pass an `EntityFactory` (`func() entity.Entity { return &T{} }`) where the signature asks for one.
2. Database/cache reads return `Entity`; type-assert to your concrete type.
3. `Timestamp` is epoch **milliseconds** (`int64`).
4. Prefer interfaces (`IDatabase`, `IDataCache`, `IMessageBus`) in signatures; only reference `NewInMemory*` in tests/wiring.
5. Close resources: `defer db.Close()`, `defer cache.Close()`, `defer bus.Close()`.

---

## Additional resources

- Repository: https://github.com/go-yaaf/yaaf-common
- License: Apache 2.0 — Go 1.24.0+
- Production adapters (PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, Kafka, Google Pub/Sub, BigQuery, ...) are separate `go-yaaf/*` modules implementing these interfaces.
